en.value: 1.1 Organize Project orientation meetings with the stakeholders including farmers, traders and decision makers at Federal, Provincial and Municipality level.1.2 Organize Technical Training on organic agriculture production and certification system for extension officers of Agriculture Knowledge Center and field technicians of the selected municipalities.1.3 Organize Technical training on organic agriculture production and certification system to the leader farmers1.4 Organize trainings to organic agri-products handlers, processors, and traders on post-harvest management including grading and packaging1.5 Organize training on farm business plan development and marketing of the organic agri- products1.6 Organize exposure visit/ to the Gov. staff to India and an in country educational tour for the Lead farmers1.7 Orientation training to the policy makers/planners of the provincial ministry and targeted municipalities1.8 Training of Trainers (TOT) for selected Gov. staff on the agriculture eco-system and organic farming practices2.1 Identify target area/sites/farmers groups for FFS conduction and organize orientation meetings.2.2 Assess training needs and develop training strategies and tools for different level (Farmers and Facilitators).2.3 Review training curricula and materials related to organic agriculture.2.4 Develop training resources, modules, curricula and manuals for FFS Facilitators training of government staff and leader farmers.2.5 Conduct Residential Training of Facilitators (ToF) for technicians (1) lead farmers (1) and Refresher courses on planning and implementation of FFS on organic agriculture production practices and certification process.2.6 Preparation and establishment of FFS activities.2.7 Implement Farmers Field School (FFS) on Organic Agriculture technologies and practices.2.8 Identification and assessment of population diversity and dynamics Natural Enemies (NEs), pollinators and soil macro and microorganisms and insects available in the FFS learning sites.2.9 Asses existing condition of the Soil health in the project sites and introduce improvement measures.2.10 Specific study on the effectiveness of bio pesticides available in the country/province for the management of soil insects and plant pests.2.11 Promotion and up-scaling the best practices against climate extremes.2.12 Document, analyze, and compile package of practices in major selected commodities tested and validated in the project sites.3.1 Organize Training on the concept and procedures of group certification.3.2 Organize training on Internal Control System (ICS) management (1 day/group) for farmers in every crop specific group.3.3 Organize Training on Internal Inspector Development on ICS-4 days.3.4 Provide follow up support in the Management of ICS for organic certification.3.5 Promotional support for marketing of certified products.4.1 Develop IEC materials (printed materials, newspaper articles, and audio/video) success stories to launch the mass media campaign for creating awareness on the organic agriculture practices and organic food consumption.4.2 Organize interaction and sharing workshops at local and national level on the project results.4.3 Establish/link with national organic agriculture information management system (OAIMS)/web portal of the Government/ MOALD.5.1 Analyze the documented practices and lessons learnt and draft the proposal.5.2 Share within the team and with province ministry and receive the inputs on the idea and implementation arrangement.5.3 Finalization of the full project proposal for submission to the Government.

en.full: Strengthening capacity of Public and Private Sector Stakeholders for promotion of organic agriculture in Karnali province of Nepal

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
